title: Executive Order 12202—Nuclear Safety Oversight Committee

flags.justification: A five-member independent citizen committee (none receiving federal salary), purely advisory in function.
Sec. 1-201 requires periodic reporting to the President, the Secretary of Energy, and the Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare — a real, multi-recipient reporting structure.
Multiple related oversight functions (NRC reforms, industry training, technical modifications, emergency planning) all coherently serve one purpose: post-Three Mile Island nuclear safety oversight.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 1-402 sets an explicit, hard termination date: September 30, 1980.
This adds an important, independent monitoring layer over the Nuclear Regulatory Commission itself, whose adequacy the Three Mile Island accident had called into question.
An independent citizen advisory body with no ongoing third-party compliance regime.
Narrowly scoped to a specific crisis-response monitoring function with an explicit termination date; no ongoing or expansive authority is claimed.
Sec. 1-209's information-access agreements with relevant agencies are a designed arrangement, not an override of the NRC's mandate.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/14 = 0.0%. A direct, independent oversight response to the Three Mile Island accident, cleanly scoped and time-limited.
